info.title: NCBI E-utilities (PubMed) API
info.description: The Entrez Programming Utilities (E-utilities) are a set of nine server-side programs that provide a stable interface into the Entrez query and database system at the National Center for Biotechnology Information (NCBI). The E-utilities use a fixed URL syntax that translates a standard set of input parameters into the values necessary for various NCBI software components to search for and retrieve the requested data. They form the backbone of PubMed programmatic access for biomedical literature search and retrieval.

get.description: Provides the number of records indexed in each field of a given database, the date of the last update, and the available links from each database. If no database parameter is supplied, EInfo returns a list of all valid Entrez databases.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Set to '2.0' to retrieve version 2.0 XML output.

parameters.name: retmode
parameters.description: Retrieval mode. Determines the format of the returned output.

200.description: Successful response with database statistics or list of databases.

get.description: Provides a list of UIDs matching a text query in a given database. Results can be stored on the History server for use in subsequent E-utility calls.

parameters.name: term
parameters.description: Entrez text query. All special characters must be URL-encoded. Spaces may be replaced by '+' signs.

parameters.name: usehistory
parameters.description: When set to 'y', ESearch will post the UIDs resulting from the search operation onto the History server so that they can be used directly in a subsequent E-utility call.

parameters.name: retmax
parameters.description: Total number of UIDs from the retrieved set to be shown in the XML output. Maximum value is 10,000 for JSON and 100,000 for XML.

parameters.name: retstart
parameters.description: Sequential index of the first UID in the retrieved set to be shown in the XML output.

parameters.name: rettype
parameters.description: Retrieval type. Set to 'uilist' for a plain list of UIDs or 'count' to return only the count.

parameters.name: retmode
parameters.description: Retrieval mode. Determines the format of the returned output.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Specifies the method used to sort UIDs in the results set. For PubMed: 'relevance', 'pub_date', 'Author', 'JournalName', etc.

parameters.name: field
parameters.description: If used, the entire search term will be limited to the specified Entrez field.

parameters.name: datetype
parameters.description: Type of date used to limit a search. Allowed values vary by database (e.g., 'mdat' for modification date, 'pdat' for publication date, 'edat' for Entrez date).

parameters.name: reldate
parameters.description: When reldate is set to an integer n, the search returns only those items that have a date specified by datetype within the last n days.

parameters.name: mindate
parameters.description: Minimum date for date range limit. Format: YYYY/MM/DD or YYYY/MM or YYYY.

parameters.name: maxdate
parameters.description: Maximum date for date range limit. Format: YYYY/MM/DD or YYYY/MM or YYYY.

parameters.name: idtype
parameters.description: Specifies the type of identifier to return for sequence databases. Set to 'acc' to return accession.version identifiers.

200.description: Successful search response with list of UIDs.

post.description: Accepts a list of UIDs from a given database, stores the set on the History Server, and responds with a query_key and WebEnv value for use in subsequent E-utility calls.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: UID list. Either a single UID or a comma-delimited list of UIDs.

200.description: Successful response with QueryKey and WebEnv values.

get.description: Returns document summaries (DocSums) for a list of input UIDs or for a set of UIDs stored on the Entrez History server.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: UID list. Either a single UID or a comma-delimited list of UIDs.

parameters.name: retstart
parameters.description: Sequential index of the first DocSum to be retrieved.

parameters.name: retmax
parameters.description: Total number of DocSums from the input set to be retrieved. Maximum of 10,000.

parameters.name: retmode
parameters.description: Retrieval mode. Determines the format of the returned output.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Set to '2.0' to retrieve version 2.0 ESummary XML.

get.description: Returns formatted data records for a list of input UIDs or for a set of UIDs stored on the Entrez History server. Supports multiple output formats depending on the database.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: UID list. Either a single UID or a comma-delimited list of UIDs.

parameters.name: rettype
parameters.description: Retrieval type. Specifies the record view returned. For PubMed: 'abstract', 'medline', 'uilist', 'docsum'. For sequences: 'fasta', 'gb', 'gp', 'native', etc.

parameters.name: retmode
parameters.description: Retrieval mode. Determines the format of the returned output. Common values: 'xml', 'text', 'asn.1'.

parameters.name: retstart
parameters.description: Sequential index of the first record to be retrieved.

parameters.name: retmax
parameters.description: Total number of records from the input set to be retrieved. Maximum of 10,000.

parameters.name: strand
parameters.description: Strand of DNA to retrieve (sequence databases only). Values: 1 (plus) or 2 (minus).

parameters.name: seq_start
parameters.description: First sequence base to retrieve (sequence databases only).

parameters.name: seq_stop
parameters.description: Last sequence base to retrieve (sequence databases only).

parameters.name: complexity
parameters.description: Data content to return (sequence databases only). 0=entire blob, 1=bioseq, 2=minimal bioseq-set, 3=minimal nuc-prot, 4=minimal pub-set.

parameters.name: idtype
parameters.description: Specifies the type of identifier to return for sequence databases. Set to 'acc' to return accession.version.

200.description: Successful response with formatted data records.

get.description: Returns UIDs linked to a set of input UIDs in either the same or a different Entrez database. Can also return computed neighbors, LinkOut URLs, and links stored on the History server.

parameters.name: dbfrom
parameters.description: Database containing the input UIDs.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: UID list. Either a single UID or a comma-delimited list. May be repeated for 'By Id' mode.

parameters.name: cmd
parameters.description: ELink command mode. Controls the type of links returned.

parameters.name: linkname
parameters.description: Name of the Entrez link to retrieve. Every link in Entrez is given a name of the form dbfrom_db_subset.

parameters.name: term
parameters.description: Entrez query used to limit the output set of linked UIDs.

parameters.name: holding
parameters.description: Name of LinkOut provider. Only URLs for the LinkOut provider specified will be returned.

parameters.name: datetype
parameters.description: Type of date used to limit a link operation.

parameters.name: reldate
parameters.description: Limit link results to items within the last n days.

parameters.name: mindate
parameters.description: Minimum date for date range limit. Format: YYYY/MM/DD.

parameters.name: maxdate
parameters.description: Maximum date for date range limit. Format: YYYY/MM/DD.

parameters.name: retmode
parameters.description: Retrieval mode. Determines the format of the returned output.

parameters.name: idtype
parameters.description: Specifies the type of identifier to return for sequence databases.

200.description: Successful response with linked UIDs or link information.

get.summary: Global Entrez search across all databases
get.description: Provides the number of records retrieved in all Entrez databases by a single text query.

parameters.name: term
parameters.description: Entrez text query.

parameters.name: retmode
parameters.description: Retrieval mode.

200.description: Successful response with hit counts per database.

get.description: Provides spelling suggestions for a text query in a given database.

parameters.name: term
parameters.description: Entrez text query.

get.description: Retrieves PubMed IDs (PMIDs) that correspond to a set of input citation strings. Each input citation string must include the journal title, publication year, volume, first page, and author name.

parameters.name: db
parameters.description: Database to search. Only 'pubmed' is supported.

parameters.name: retmode
parameters.description: Retrieval mode. Only 'xml' is supported.

parameters.name: bdata
parameters.description: Citation strings. Each citation string has the format: journal_title|year|volume|first_page|author_name|your_key|. Multiple citation strings should be separated by a carriage return character (%0D).

200.description: Successful response with citation strings appended with corresponding PMIDs.

db_required.name: db
db_required.description: Target Entrez database. Common values include: 'pubmed', 'protein', 'nuccore', 'nucleotide', 'gene', 'genome', 'pmc', 'snp', 'mesh', 'books', etc. Use EInfo without a db parameter to get a full list.

db_optional.name: db
db_optional.description: Target Entrez database. If not provided, returns a list of all valid Entrez databases.

api_key.name: api_key
api_key.description: API key from an NCBI account. Without an API key, requests are limited to 3 per second. With an API key, up to 10 requests per second are allowed.

tool.name: tool
tool.description: Name of application making the E-utility call. Value must be a string with no internal spaces.

email.name: email
email.description: E-mail address of the E-utility user. Providing a valid email helps NCBI contact you if there are problems.

WebEnv.name: WebEnv
WebEnv.description: Web environment string returned from a previous ESearch, EPost, or ELink call. Used to access UIDs stored on the History server.

query_key.name: query_key
query_key.description: Integer query key returned by a previous ESearch, EPost, or ELink call. Represents a specific set of UIDs stored on the History server.

apiKey.name: api_key
apiKey.description: NCBI API key. Register at https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/account/ to obtain one. Increases rate limit from 3 to 10 requests/second.
